steps.title: Understand what f(0) represents
steps.content: The notation $f(0)$ asks for the y-value when $x = 0$. On a graph, this is found by locating the y-axis (the vertical line where $x = 0$) and reading where the curve intersects it.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: Tracing up the y-axis to where the curve crosses it, the intersection point is at $y = -6$. Therefore, $f(0) = -6$, which is Choice A — -6.
